faq.question: Why are some coffee machine programmes like Hot Water or Coffee Jug missing from HomeKit?
faq.answer: The absence of specific programmes, such as Hot Water or Coffee Jug, is due to limitations within the official Home Connect API. The plugin can only expose functionality that the API makes available for a specific appliance model. Many specialised programmes are currently excluded from the Home Connect API specification for coffee makers. You can verify the list of supported programmes in the [Home Connect developer documentation](https://developer.home-connect.com/docs/coffee-maker/supported_programs_and_options). If a feature available on your physical appliance is missing from the API, you may wish to contact Home Connect support directly to request its inclusion in future API updates.
faq.semantic_abstract: Home Connect API technical constraints prevent the exposure of specific coffee machine programmes such as Hot Water or Coffee Jug to HomeKit. These specialised functions are excluded from the official API specification for certain Siemens and Bosch models, meaning they cannot be mapped to HomeKit services by the plugin.
faq.alternative_resolution: The plugin documentation could be updated to include a section on API-level limitations, explaining that the available HomeKit programmes are strictly bound by the Home Connect API's supported feature list for each appliance category.
